marketOverview: The Super Week central bank sweep has reshuffled the G10 landscape. EUR and GBP pivoted hawkish (ECB April hike talk, BOE 9-0 unanimous hold with cut language removed), while JPY found late-week haven demand as US equities corrected and carry trades unwound. The Strait of Hormuz remains effectively closed with Brent above $100/bbl, but the market is now differentiating more carefully between energy importers with hawkish central banks (EUR, GBP) and those without (JPY, CHF). AUD holds its post-RBA hike gains. CAD is supported by oil but constrained by the jobs shock. CHF faces explicit SNB intervention resistance. NZD remains the most exposed high-beta currency with no domestic catalyst.

currencies.name: US Dollar
currencies.rationale: The Dollar lost its clean bullish narrative after the FOMC held 4-1 (Waller dissented for a hike) and Powell acknowledged the stagflation dilemma without committing to further tightening. DXY softened to the 99.99-100.31 range as EUR and GBP both rallied on their own hawkish CB pivots. The Dollar retains safe-haven and liquidity appeal, but with peers now repricing higher for longer too, the relative rate advantage has narrowed. Flash PMIs (Tue) and PCE (Fri) are the key catalysts — a hot PCE could restore the bullish case, while a miss would confirm the neutral stance.
drivers: FOMC held 4-1 (Waller dissented for hike); Powell cautious on stagflation dilemma
DXY softened to 99.99-100.31 as EUR and GBP rallied on hawkish CB pivots
Relative rate advantage narrowed: ECB, BOE, SNB all shifted hawkish
Safe-haven and liquidity premium still intact but no clean bullish catalyst
Flash PMIs (Tue) and PCE (Fri) are the key catalysts this week

currencies.name: Euro
currencies.rationale: EUR pivoted from Weak Bearish to Weak Bullish after the ECB delivered a hawkish hold: HICP 2026 forecast upgraded to 2.6% (from 1.9%), GDP 2026 cut to 0.9% (from 1.2%), and source reports flagged April hike talk with June as the more likely move. Barclays and JPMorgan both revised to forecast an April hike. EUR/USD recovered from 7-month lows near 1.1466. The energy import headwind remains but the rates repricing now dominates the narrative. Flash PMIs (Tue) and German IFO (Wed) are the key catalysts — a beat would reinforce the hawkish pivot story.
drivers: ECB hawkish hold: HICP 2026 upgraded to 2.6%, April hike talk in source reports
Barclays and JPMorgan now forecast April ECB hike (June more likely per sources)
EUR/USD recovered from 7-month lows near 1.1466 — rates repricing dominates
Energy import headwind remains (Brent $100+) but no longer the primary driver
Flash PMIs (Tue) and German IFO (Wed) are the key catalysts this week

currencies.name: British Pound
currencies.rationale: Sterling reversed from Weak Bearish to Weak Bullish after the BOE delivered a hawkish shock: 9-0 unanimous hold (exp. 7-2), cut language removed, and Mann signalling a potential hike. Goldman Sachs now sees the BOE on hold all 2026 with easing starting 2027. UK Jobs beat massively: Employment Change +84k (vs -4k exp), Unemployment 5.2% (in line), though Earnings ex-Bonus 3.8% (below 4.0% exp) showed some wage softening. UK CPI (Wed) is the key event — if it beats, the April hike probability rises sharply. UK Retail Sales (Fri) and Flash PMIs (Tue) complete the week.
drivers: BOE hawkish shock: 9-0 unanimous hold, cut language removed, Mann signals hike
Goldman now sees BOE on hold all 2026; easing starting 2027
UK Jobs beat: Employment +84k (vs -4k exp); Unemployment 5.2%
Earnings ex-Bonus 3.8% (below 4.0% exp) — wages softening, limits hike urgency
UK CPI (Wed) is the key catalyst — beat would raise April hike probability sharply

currencies.name: Japanese Yen
currencies.rationale: JPY upgraded from Bearish to Neutral after a late-week reversal: as US equity futures fell sharply (Russell 2000 into correction territory), the Yen finally attracted defensive demand as carry trades unwound. BOJ held 8-1 with Ueda hawkish on wages — SME wage momentum improving, June hike a live possibility. USD/JPY slumped briefly below 158.00. The tension between haven demand and energy vulnerability remains unresolved, but the late-week shift shows the Yen can outperform when fear shifts from inflation to growth destruction. National Core CPI (Mon) and BOJ Core CPI (Thu) are the key events.
drivers: BOJ held 8-1; Ueda hawkish on wages — SME momentum improving, June hike live
Late-week haven demand as US equities corrected and carry trades unwound
USD/JPY slumped below 158.00 — intervention zone pressure reduced
Energy vulnerability remains: Japan imports 90%+ of crude from Middle East
National Core CPI (Mon) and BOJ Core CPI (Thu) are the key events

currencies.name: Australian Dollar
currencies.rationale: AUD holds its Weak Bullish stance after the RBA delivered the expected 25bps hike to 4.10% on Tuesday. Markets now price a coin-flip for a third hike in May, keeping the rates support intact. AUD/USD held the post-RBA gains despite a late-week pullback driven by sharp losses in gold and copper. The energy exporter advantage remains supportive. AUD CPI (Wed 01:30 FFT) is the key event — a hot print would raise May hike probability and push AUD higher.
drivers: RBA hiked to 4.10% as expected; coin-flip for 3rd hike in May priced in
Energy exporter advantage: Australia benefits from elevated oil and LNG prices
AUD/USD held post-RBA gains despite late-week gold and copper weakness
China data remains supportive (Jan-Feb trade beats across all metrics)
AUD CPI (Wed 01:30 FFT) is the key catalyst — hot print raises May hike odds

currencies.name: Canadian Dollar
currencies.rationale: CAD retains its Weak Bullish stance on oil support (WTI $95.55/bbl, Brent $108.65/bbl) despite the BOC holding and the -83.9k jobs shock from last week. The commodity channel provides a floor, but the labor market deterioration and BOC caution limit the upside. USD/CAD remains elevated near 1.3700 after reclaiming the 50-day MA. No major CAD events this week — the currency will trade as a petro-proxy, tracking oil prices and global risk sentiment.
drivers: Oil support: WTI $95.55/bbl, Brent $108.65/bbl — commodity channel intact
BOC held; inflation constraint from triple-digit oil limits easing path
Jobs shock -83.9k last week limits CAD upside; USD/CAD near 1.3700
No major CAD events this week — pure petro-proxy, tracks oil and risk sentiment
Iraq-Kurdistan export deal and Gulf supply disruption keep oil bid

currencies.name: Swiss Franc
currencies.rationale: CHF shifted from Neutral to Weak Bearish after the SNB delivered explicit intervention language: 'willingness to intervene has increased' to counter 'rapid and excessive appreciation'. Chairman Schlegel noted CHF had appreciated 2.5% on a trade-weighted basis since December. EUR/CHF rose towards 0.9100 post-SNB. The Franc retains structural haven characteristics but official resistance is now too explicit to ignore. SNB Schlegel speech (Tue) and SNB Quarterly Bulletin (Wed) are the key events — any softening of the intervention language would be CHF positive.
drivers: SNB explicit intervention: 'willingness to intervene has increased' — Schlegel
CHF appreciated 2.5% trade-weighted since December; SNB sees this as excessive
EUR/CHF rose towards 0.9100 post-SNB; Franc's haven bid now officially capped
SECO cut 2026 GDP to 1.0%; PPI deflation -2.7% YoY — SNB has domestic cover
SNB Schlegel speech (Tue) and SNB Quarterly Bulletin (Wed) are key events

currencies.name: New Zealand Dollar
currencies.rationale: NZD remains the most exposed G10 currency — a high-beta risk proxy with no domestic hawkish catalyst, a current account deficit of -5.98B Q4 (miss vs -4.78B exp), and no RBNZ meeting until April. RBNZ Governor Breman speaks Tuesday (02:00 FFT) — if she signals a soft policy path, NZD pressure increases. The Kiwi has no rates support to offset the global risk-off environment, and AUD continues to outperform on the RBA hike story. AUD/NZD remains biased higher.
drivers: High-beta risk proxy: no domestic hawkish catalyst unlike AUD's RBA story
Current Account Q4 -5.98B (vs -4.78B exp) — wider deficit than expected
No RBNZ meeting until April — policy support absent
RBNZ Governor Breman speech (Tue 02:00 FFT) — soft tone would add pressure
AUD continues to outperform on RBA hike story; AUD/NZD biased higher

long.rationale: Weak Bullish AUD (RBA hike delivered, May hike priced) vs Bearish NZD (no RBNZ, current account miss, high-beta)
Weak Bullish EUR (ECB hawkish pivot, April hike talk) vs Weak Bearish CHF (SNB explicit intervention resistance)
Weak Bullish EUR (ECB hawkish hold, rates repricing) vs Bearish NZD (no domestic catalyst, high-beta)
Weak Bullish GBP (BOE 9-0 hawkish hold, cut language removed) vs Weak Bearish CHF (SNB intervention cap)
Weak Bullish GBP (BOE hawkish pivot, strong jobs data) vs Bearish NZD (no RBNZ, current account miss)

short.rationale: Bearish NZD (high-beta, no catalyst, current account miss) vs Weak Bullish CAD (oil support, petro-proxy)
